articles.title: Tactical fluidity that makes PSG so impressive - and such a danger to Bayern
articles.summary: Despite boasting squads that have included the likes of Lionel Messi, Neymar and Kylian Mbappe, it is the current Paris St-Germain side that is playing the club's most impressive football.

articles.title: 'Tottenham messed up' - Spurs win at last, but is it too late?

articles.summary: Andoni Iraola, Marco Silva and Xabi Alonso are among the managers Chelsea are discussing as potential replacements for Liam Rosenior this summer.

articles.summary: Denver Nuggets mascot Rocky hits a backwards halfcourt shot from the top of two ladders!

articles.title: All but back in Champions League - yet big decisions loom at Man Utd
articles.summary: Barring an unlikely sequence of results, Old Trafford will host Champions League football again next season, but for those running the club, their most significant work is about to begin.

articles.title: Man Utd survive Brentford fightback to earn crucial win

articles.title: Carrick hails 'big three points' after beating Brentford
articles.summary: Manchester United interim boss Michael Carrick says that his side's goal is "to finish as high up the league as possible" following their 2-1 win against Brentford at Old Trafford.

articles.title: Andrews 'bitterly disappointed' after Brentford lose at Man Utd
articles.summary: Brentford boss Keith Andrews says his side aren't "playing fearful" football and that he doesn't want to be that sort of team after a 2-1 loss to Manchester United in the Premier League.
